name: Real Response Comparison Search Pass

search_queries.result_count: screened first result page for targeted query
screened first result page for targeted query

search_queries.query: "LLM respondent" human response distribution item evaluation
search_queries.result_count: screened first result page for targeted query
screened first result page for targeted query

search_queries.query: "Can Large Language Models Emulate Human Performance" educational assessments
search_queries.result_count: reviewed abstract/metadata page; full text was not verified in this pass

search_queries.query: "Using LLMs to Simulate Students' Responses to Exam Questions"
search_queries.result_count: screened as scope-adjacent exam-question simulation evidence

inclusion_criteria: The source compares synthetic or simulated learner/student responses with real student response data, real response distributions, real response scores, or ground-truth real dialogue turns.
The comparator should share the same items, prompts, essays, questions, grade band, domain, or dialogue context closely enough to support an interpretable comparison.
Direct findings for the baseline require accessible full text and source locators beyond an abstract-only page.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude sources that only generate synthetic responses without evaluating against real student responses or real respondent distributions.
Exclude sources where the comparator is item difficulty, model labels, or theoretical answer correctness rather than empirical real student responses, unless explicitly retained as boundary evidence.
Exclude abstract-only or paywalled sources from direct synthesis support until full text is obtained or the finding is explicitly limited to abstract-level discovery.
dedupe_method: Deduplicated by title, DOI, arXiv identifier, venue metadata, and canonical source page.

screening_counts.records_identified: at least 35 web and primary-source records across targeted queries

screening_decisions.title: Agent4Edu: Generating Learner Response Data by Generative Agents for Intelligent Education Systems
screening_decisions.reason: Compares generated learner responses with human learner response data from EduData using consistency/discrepancy metrics.

screening_decisions.title: Calibrating Generative AI to Produce Realistic Essays for Data Augmentation
screening_decisions.reason: Uses real student essays as source/comparator and reports prompt-specific agreement, weighted kappa, score distribution, and human detection outcomes.

screening_decisions.title: Generative Students: Using LLM-Simulated Student Profiles to Support Question Item Evaluation
screening_decisions.reason: Compares GPT-4 simulated student responses with real student responses to the same item-evaluation setting, including profile-rule response alignment and real-response item signals.

screening_decisions.title: Can LLMs Reliably Simulate Real Students' Abilities in Mathematics and Reading Comprehension?
screening_decisions.reason: Directly compares LLM proxy-student NAEP item answers with real NAEP response patterns on an IRT ability scale.

screening_decisions.title: Leveraging LLM Respondents for Item Evaluation: A Psychometric Analysis
screening_decisions.reason: Compares LLM respondent distributions and item calibrations with undergraduate human responses to the same College Algebra items.

screening_decisions.title: Simulated Students in Tutoring Dialogues: Substance or Illusion?
screening_decisions.reason: Benchmarks prompted and post-trained simulated-student dialogue turns against real math tutoring dialogue turns across fidelity metrics.

screening_decisions.title: Who Am I? History-Aware Profiles for Student Simulation in Tutoring Dialogues
screening_decisions.reason: Compares history/profile-conditioned simulated student turns with ground-truth future real student dialogue turns and prior real response histories.

screening_decisions.title: Can Large Language Models Emulate Human Performance on Educational Assessments?
screening_decisions.reason: The abstract page appears directly relevant to human-vs-LLM assessment comparison, but full text was not verified in this pass, so it is not used as direct support.

screening_decisions.title: Using LLMs to Simulate Students' Responses to Exam Questions
screening_decisions.reason: Full-text exam-question simulation evidence, but the targeted pass did not identify an empirical real-student response distribution comparator strong enough for this node.

screening_decisions.title: A Framework for Designing an AI Chatbot to Support Scientific Argumentation
screening_decisions.reason: Uses real examples to condition synthetic inputs, but does not validate generated inputs against a real response distribution.

screening_decisions.title: DRESS: Dataset for Rubric-based Essay Scoring on ELL Writings
screening_decisions.reason: Useful boundary source for synthetic/corrupted writing and rubric scoring, but not a direct synthetic-versus-real response comparison source for this pass.

screening_summary: Targeted bootstrap screening included seven full-text sources that compare generated learner/student responses with real student data: learner response records, real-source essays, real MCQ item responses, real NAEP response patterns, undergraduate College Algebra responses, and real tutoring dialogue turns. Abstract-only or comparator-mismatched sources were screened but not used as direct support.
limitations: This was a targeted bootstrap pass, not a systematic review.
Included comparators vary substantially: same source essays, aggregate response distributions, IRT ability scales, item-parameter correlations, and dialogue-turn matching metrics.
Some included evidence is current preprint evidence and should be monitored for revision.
The pass intentionally does not use abstract-only sources as direct evidence.
